---
title: "trt_het_wass_vignette"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
# WASS INFERENCE START 
# Gist: need a command which takes X, A, Y vectors, returns the following:
# the heterogineity decomposition, estimated Psi^2 wasserstein effect across 
# covariates, testing for each term(?) [READ THE TESTING SECTION!]


# Helper, makes design matrix
make_design_matrix <- function(X, add_intercept = TRUE) {
  if (is.data.frame(X)) {
    non_numeric <- !vapply(X, is.numeric, logical(1))
    if (any(non_numeric)) {
      stop(
        "X must be numeric. Expand factors and construct nonlinear bases ",
        "before passing X to this function."
      )
    }
  }
  
  X <- as.matrix(X)
  storage.mode(X) <- "double"

  if (length(X) == 0L || nrow(X) == 0L) {
    stop("X must contain at least one observation.")
  }

  if (any(!is.finite(X))) {
    stop("X contains non-finite values.")
  }

  if (is.null(colnames(X))) {
    colnames(X) <- paste0("x", seq_len(ncol(X)))
  }

  if (add_intercept) {
    X <- cbind("(Intercept)" = rep(1, nrow(X)), X)
  }

  X
}

# For checking u \in (0,1)
validate_u_grid <- function(u) {
  if (!is.numeric(u) || length(u) < 3L) {
    stop("u must be a numeric vector with at least three interior grid values.")
  }

  if (any(!is.finite(u)) || any(u <= 0) || any(u >= 1)) {
    stop("All grid values u must lie strictly inside (0, 1).")
  }

  if (any(diff(u) <= 0)) {
    stop("u must be strictly increasing.")
  }

  invisible(TRUE)
}


# Riemann rectangle heights 
quadrature_weights <- function(u,
                               rule = c("trapezoid", "midpoint")) {
  validate_u_grid(u)
  rule <- match.arg(rule)

  M <- length(u)

  if (rule == "midpoint") {
    # Partitions [0, (u1+u2)/2], ..., [(uM-1+uM)/2, 1].
    boundaries <- c(
      0,
      (u[-1L] + u[-M]) / 2,
      1
    )
    w <- diff(boundaries)
  } else {
    # Trapezoid rule with constant extrapolation from u_1 to 0
    # and from u_M to 1.
    w <- numeric(M)

    w[1L] <- u[1L] + (u[2L] - u[1L]) / 2
    w[M] <- (1 - u[M]) + (u[M] - u[M - 1L]) / 2

    if (M > 2L) {
      w[2L:(M - 1L)] <- (
        (u[2L:(M - 1L)] - u[1L:(M - 2L)]) / 2 +
          (u[3L:M] - u[2L:(M - 1L)]) / 2
      )
    }
  }

  if (any(w <= 0) || abs(sum(w) - 1) > 1e-10) {
    stop("Quadrature weights are invalid.")
  }

  w
}

# Grid integral approximation
integrate_u <- function(H, w) {
  if (is.null(dim(H))) {
    if (length(H) != length(w)) {
      stop("Vector H and quadrature weights w have incompatible lengths.")
    }
    return(sum(H * w))
  }

  H <- as.matrix(H)

  if (ncol(H) != length(w)) {
    stop("The number of columns of H must equal length(w).")
  }

  rowSums(sweep(H, 2L, w, FUN = "*"))
}

standard_error_from_if <- function(influence_values) {
  influence_values <- as.numeric(influence_values)

  if (length(influence_values) < 2L) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  sqrt(stats::var(influence_values) / length(influence_values))
}
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Monotone rearrangement of quantile curves
#
# For a fixed x, the rearranged curve is the quantile function of
# Q_hat(U | x), where U is approximately Uniform(0,1).
#
# This implementation allows non-equally-spaced u grids by using quadrature
# weights in the empirical distribution of Q_hat(U | x).
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

weighted_rearrange_row <- function(q, u, w) {
  ord <- order(q, na.last = NA)

  if (length(ord) != length(q)) {
    stop("Quantile predictions contain missing values.")
  }

  q_sorted <- q[ord]
  cumulative_weight <- cumsum(w[ord])

  index <- vapply(
    u,
    FUN = function(uu) {
      hit <- which(cumulative_weight >= uu - sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
      if (length(hit) == 0L) length(q_sorted) else hit[1L]
    },
    FUN.VALUE = integer(1L)
  )

  q_sorted[index]
}


rearrange_quantile_matrix <- function(Q, u, w) {
  Q <- as.matrix(Q)

  if (ncol(Q) != length(u) || length(u) != length(w)) {
    stop("Q, u, and w have incompatible dimensions.")
  }

  out <- t(vapply(
    seq_len(nrow(Q)),
    FUN = function(i) weighted_rearrange_row(Q[i, ], u = u, w = w),
    FUN.VALUE = numeric(length(u))
  ))

  colnames(out) <- paste0("u_", format(u, trim = TRUE))
  out
}
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Conditional density estimation at Q_a(u | x)
#
# Since dQ(u | x)/du = 1 / f(Q(u | x) | x), estimate density by a finite
# difference approximation to the derivative of the rearranged quantile curve.
#
# A slope floor is required because a finite grid and rearrangement can produce
# flat local stretches. This is a numerical regularization parameter and should
# be included in sensitivity analysis.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

density_from_quantile_grid <- function(Q,
                                       u,
                                       slope_floor,
                                       density_cap = Inf) {
  Q <- as.matrix(Q)
  validate_u_grid(u)

  if (ncol(Q) != length(u)) {
    stop("Q and u have incompatible dimensions.")
  }

  if (!is.numeric(slope_floor) ||
      length(slope_floor) != 1L ||
      !is.finite(slope_floor) ||
      slope_floor <= 0) {
    stop("slope_floor must be a positive finite scalar.")
  }

  M <- length(u)
  dQ_du <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = nrow(Q), ncol = M)

  dQ_du[, 1L] <- (Q[, 2L] - Q[, 1L]) / (u[2L] - u[1L])
  dQ_du[, M] <- (Q[, M] - Q[, M - 1L]) / (u[M] - u[M - 1L])

  if (M > 2L) {
    for (m in 2L:(M - 1L)) {
      dQ_du[, m] <- (Q[, m + 1L] - Q[, m - 1L]) /
        (u[m + 1L] - u[m - 1L])
    }
  }

  dQ_du <- pmax(dQ_du, slope_floor)

  density <- 1 / dQ_du

  if (is.finite(density_cap)) {
    density <- pmin(density, density_cap)
  }

  density
}
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Default nuisance estimators
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

fit_quantile_grid <- function(X, Y, u, method = "br") {
  X <- as.matrix(X)
  Y <- as.numeric(Y)
  validate_u_grid(u)

  if (nrow(X) != length(Y)) {
    stop("X and Y have incompatible dimensions.")
  }

  p <- ncol(X)

  B <- vapply(
    u,
    FUN = function(uu) {
      fit <- tryCatch(
        quantreg::rq.fit(
          x = X,
          y = Y,
          tau = uu,
          method = method
        ),
        error = function(e) {
          stop(
            "Quantile regression failed at u = ", uu,
            ". Original error: ", conditionMessage(e)
          )
        }
      )

      coef <- fit$coefficients
      if (is.null(coef)) {
        coef <- fit$coef
      }

      if (length(coef) != p || any(!is.finite(coef))) {
        stop("Quantile regression returned invalid coefficients at u = ", uu)
      }

      as.numeric(coef)
    },
    FUN.VALUE = numeric(p)
  )

  list(
    coef = t(B),
    u = u
  )
}


predict_quantile_grid <- function(fit, X_new) {
  X_new <- as.matrix(X_new)

  if (ncol(X_new) != ncol(fit$coef)) {
    stop("X_new has the wrong number of columns.")
  }

  X_new %*% t(fit$coef)
}


default_nuisance_fns <- function(qr_method = "br") {
  list(
    fit_propensity = function(X, A) {
      fit <- stats::glm.fit(
        x = X,
        y = A,
        family = stats::binomial()
      )

      if (anyNA(fit$coefficients)) {
        stop(
          "The default logistic propensity model is rank deficient. ",
          "Supply a different propensity learner or simplify X."
        )
      }

      list(coef = fit$coefficients)
    },

    predict_propensity = function(fit, X_new) {
      as.numeric(stats::plogis(drop(X_new %*% fit$coef)))
    },

    fit_mean = function(X, Y) {
      fit <- stats::lm.fit(x = X, y = Y)

      if (anyNA(fit$coefficients)) {
        stop(
          "The default linear outcome model is rank deficient. ",
          "Supply a different outcome-regression learner or simplify X."
        )
      }

      list(coef = fit$coefficients)
    },

    predict_mean = function(fit, X_new) {
      as.numeric(drop(X_new %*% fit$coef))
    },

    fit_quantiles = function(X, Y, u) {
      fit_quantile_grid(X = X, Y = Y, u = u, method = qr_method)
    },

    predict_quantiles = function(fit, X_new) {
      predict_quantile_grid(fit, X_new)
    }
  )
}
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fold construction
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

make_stratified_folds <- function(A, K = 5L, seed = NULL) {
  A <- as.integer(A)

  if (!all(A %in% c(0L, 1L))) {
    stop("A must be coded 0 and 1.")
  }

  if (!is.null(seed)) {
    set.seed(seed)
  }

  group_sizes <- table(A)

  if (K < 2L || min(group_sizes) < K) {
    stop(
      "K must be at least 2 and cannot exceed the number of observations ",
      "in either treatment arm."
    )
  }

  fold <- integer(length(A))

  for (a in c(0L, 1L)) {
    index <- which(A == a)

    fold[index] <- sample(
      rep(seq_len(K), length.out = length(index)),
      size = length(index),
      replace = FALSE
    )
  }

  fold
}
```


```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Quantile scores eta_a(Z, u)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

outcome_indicator_matrix <- function(Y, Q) {
  sweep(
    Q,
    MARGIN = 1L,
    STATS = Y,
    FUN = function(q_row, y_i) as.numeric(y_i <= q_row)
  )
}


compute_quantile_scores <- function(A,
                                    Y,
                                    u,
                                    Q1,
                                    Q0,
                                    pi_hat,
                                    f1_hat,
                                    f0_hat) {
  n <- length(Y)
  M <- length(u)

  U_mat <- matrix(
    u,
    nrow = n,
    ncol = M,
    byrow = TRUE
  )

  indicator_1 <- outcome_indicator_matrix(Y, Q1)
  indicator_0 <- outcome_indicator_matrix(Y, Q0)

  eta_1 <- sweep(
    (U_mat - indicator_1) / f1_hat,
    MARGIN = 1L,
    STATS = as.numeric(A == 1L) / pi_hat,
    FUN = "*"
  )

  eta_0 <- sweep(
    (U_mat - indicator_0) / f0_hat,
    MARGIN = 1L,
    STATS = as.numeric(A == 0L) / (1 - pi_hat),
    FUN = "*"
  )

  list(
    eta1 = eta_1,
    eta0 = eta_0,
    eta_diff = eta_1 - eta_0
  )
}
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Cross-fitted nuisance estimation
#
# The returned object contains out-of-fold predictions for every observation.
# Those predictions are then used by all estimators below.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

crossfit_wasserstein_nuisance <- function(X,
                                          A,
                                          Y,
                                          u = (seq_len(99L) - 0.5) / 99,
                                          K = 5L,
                                          folds = NULL,
                                          add_intercept = TRUE,
                                          integration = c("trapezoid", "midpoint"),
                                          propensity_bound = 0.01,
                                          slope_floor = NULL,
                                          density_cap = Inf,
                                          rearrange = TRUE,
                                          nuisance_fns = default_nuisance_fns(),
                                          seed = NULL) {
  integration <- match.arg(integration)

  required_fns <- c(
    "fit_propensity",
    "predict_propensity",
    "fit_mean",
    "predict_mean",
    "fit_quantiles",
    "predict_quantiles"
  )

  if (!all(required_fns %in% names(nuisance_fns))) {
    stop(
      "nuisance_fns must contain: ",
      paste(required_fns, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }

  X <- make_design_matrix(X, add_intercept = add_intercept)
  A <- as.integer(A)
  Y <- as.numeric(Y)

  if (!all(A %in% c(0L, 1L))) {
    stop("A must be coded exactly 0 and 1.")
  }

  if (nrow(X) != length(A) || length(A) != length(Y)) {
    stop("X, A, and Y have incompatible dimensions.")
  }

  if (any(!is.finite(Y))) {
    stop("Y contains non-finite values.")
  }

  validate_u_grid(u)
  w <- quadrature_weights(u, rule = integration)

  n <- nrow(X)
  M <- length(u)

  if (is.null(folds)) {
    folds <- make_stratified_folds(A = A, K = K, seed = seed)
  } else {
    if (length(folds) != n) {
      stop("folds must have length n.")
    }

    folds <- match(folds, unique(folds))

    if (length(unique(folds)) < 2L) {
      stop("folds must contain at least two folds.")
    }
  }

  if (is.null(slope_floor)) {
    y_scale <- stats::mad(Y, constant = 1)

    if (!is.finite(y_scale) || y_scale <= 0) {
      y_scale <- stats::sd(Y)
    }

    if (!is.finite(y_scale) || y_scale <= 0) {
      y_scale <- 1
    }

    slope_floor <- 1e-6 * y_scale
  }

  Q1 <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = M)
  Q0 <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = M)

  mu1 <- rep(NA_real_, n)
  mu0 <- rep(NA_real_, n)
  pi_hat <- rep(NA_real_, n)

  for (fold_id in sort(unique(folds))) {
    test_id <- which(folds == fold_id)
    train_id <- which(folds != fold_id)

    if (length(unique(A[train_id])) != 2L) {
      stop(
        "At least one training fold lacks a treatment arm. ",
        "Reduce K or provide valid folds."
      )
    }

    X_train <- X[train_id, , drop = FALSE]
    X_test <- X[test_id, , drop = FALSE]

    A_train <- A[train_id]
    Y_train <- Y[train_id]

    # Propensity model.
    pi_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_propensity(X_train, A_train)
    pi_hat[test_id] <- nuisance_fns$predict_propensity(pi_fit, X_test)

    # Conditional mean models.
    mu1_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_mean(
      X_train[A_train == 1L, , drop = FALSE],
      Y_train[A_train == 1L]
    )

    mu0_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_mean(
      X_train[A_train == 0L, , drop = FALSE],
      Y_train[A_train == 0L]
    )

    mu1[test_id] <- nuisance_fns$predict_mean(mu1_fit, X_test)
    mu0[test_id] <- nuisance_fns$predict_mean(mu0_fit, X_test)

    # Conditional quantile models.
    q1_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_quantiles(
      X_train[A_train == 1L, , drop = FALSE],
      Y_train[A_train == 1L],
      u
    )

    q0_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_quantiles(
      X_train[A_train == 0L, , drop = FALSE],
      Y_train[A_train == 0L],
      u
    )

    q1_test <- as.matrix(nuisance_fns$predict_quantiles(q1_fit, X_test))
    q0_test <- as.matrix(nuisance_fns$predict_quantiles(q0_fit, X_test))

    if (nrow(q1_test) != length(test_id) || ncol(q1_test) != M ||
        nrow(q0_test) != length(test_id) || ncol(q0_test) != M) {
      stop(
        "The quantile prediction function must return an ",
        "n_test by length(u) matrix."
      )
    }

    if (rearrange) {
      q1_test <- rearrange_quantile_matrix(q1_test, u = u, w = w)
      q0_test <- rearrange_quantile_matrix(q0_test, u = u, w = w)
    }

    Q1[test_id, ] <- q1_test
    Q0[test_id, ] <- q0_test
  }

  if (any(!is.finite(pi_hat)) ||
      any(!is.finite(mu1)) ||
      any(!is.finite(mu0)) ||
      any(!is.finite(Q1)) ||
      any(!is.finite(Q0))) {
    stop("Cross-fitting produced non-finite nuisance predictions.")
  }

  if (!is.numeric(propensity_bound) ||
      length(propensity_bound) != 1L ||
      propensity_bound <= 0 ||
      propensity_bound >= 0.5) {
    stop("propensity_bound must lie strictly between 0 and 0.5.")
  }

  pi_hat <- pmin(
    pmax(pi_hat, propensity_bound),
    1 - propensity_bound
  )

  f1_hat <- density_from_quantile_grid(
    Q = Q1,
    u = u,
    slope_floor = slope_floor,
    density_cap = density_cap
  )

  f0_hat <- density_from_quantile_grid(
    Q = Q0,
    u = u,
    slope_floor = slope_floor,
    density_cap = density_cap
  )

  scores <- compute_quantile_scores(
    A = A,
    Y = Y,
    u = u,
    Q1 = Q1,
    Q0 = Q0,
    pi_hat = pi_hat,
    f1_hat = f1_hat,
    f0_hat = f0_hat
  )

  delta <- Q1 - Q0

  tau_x_quantile <- integrate_u(delta, w)
  tau_x_mean <- mu1 - mu0

  R_tau <- (
    A / pi_hat * (Y - mu1) -
      (1 - A) / (1 - pi_hat) * (Y - mu0)
  )

  bar_delta <- colMeans(delta)
  tau_quantile <- integrate_u(bar_delta, w)

  structure(
    list(
      X = X,
      A = A,
      Y = Y,
      u = u,
      w = w,
      folds = folds,

      pi_hat = pi_hat,
      mu1 = mu1,
      mu0 = mu0,

      Q1 = Q1,
      Q0 = Q0,
      f1_hat = f1_hat,
      f0_hat = f0_hat,

      delta = delta,
      eta1 = scores$eta1,
      eta0 = scores$eta0,
      eta_diff = scores$eta_diff,

      tau_x_quantile = tau_x_quantile,
      tau_x_mean = tau_x_mean,
      R_tau = R_tau,

      bar_delta = bar_delta,
      tau_quantile = tau_quantile,

      slope_floor = slope_floor,
      propensity_bound = propensity_bound
    ),
    class = "wasserstein_nuisance"
  )
}
```


```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Term 1: Conditional Wasserstein effect psi^2(X_i)
#
# For the observed X_i, this returns the cross-fitted estimate
#
#   psi_hat^2(X_i) = sum_m w_m Delta_hat(X_i, u_m)^2.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Conditional Wasserstein effect
estimate_psi2_x <- function(cf) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_nuisance"))

  integrate_u(cf$delta^2, cf$w)
}


# Average Wasserstein effect

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Overall Wasserstein effect Psi^2
#
# Psi_hat^2 =
#   n^{-1} sum_i int [
#     Delta_hat(X_i,u)^2 +
#     2 Delta_hat(X_i,u) {eta_hat_1(Z_i,u) - eta_hat_0(Z_i,u)}
#   ] du.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

estimate_Psi2 <- function(cf) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_nuisance"))

  plug_in_i <- integrate_u(cf$delta^2, cf$w)

  correction_i <- 2 * integrate_u(
    cf$delta * cf$eta_diff,
    cf$w
  )

  contribution_i <- plug_in_i + correction_i
  estimate <- mean(contribution_i)

  list(
    estimate = estimate,
    plug_in = mean(plug_in_i),
    correction = mean(correction_i),
    contribution = contribution_i,
    influence = contribution_i - estimate,
    se = standard_error_from_if(contribution_i - estimate)
  )
}





# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Average treatment effect tau by AIPW
#
# tau_hat =
#   n^{-1} sum_i [
#     mu_hat_1(X_i) - mu_hat_0(X_i) + R_hat_tau(Z_i)
#   ].
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

estimate_tau_aipw <- function(cf) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_nuisance"))

  contribution_i <- (cf$mu1 - cf$mu0) + cf$R_tau
  estimate <- mean(contribution_i)

  list(
    estimate = estimate,
    contribution = contribution_i,
    influence = contribution_i - estimate,
    se = standard_error_from_if(contribution_i - estimate)
  )
}


get_tau_x <- function(cf,
                      tau_x_method = c("quantile", "outcome_mean")) {
  tau_x_method <- match.arg(tau_x_method)

  if (tau_x_method == "quantile") {
    return(cf$tau_x_quantile)
  }
  
  cf$tau_x_mean
}





# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# V[tau(X)]
#
# V_hat[tau(X)] =
#   n^{-1} sum_i [
#     {tau_hat(X_i) - tau_hat}^2 +
#     2 {tau_hat(X_i) - tau_hat} R_hat_tau(Z_i)
#   ].
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
estimate_V_tauX <- function(cf,
                            tau_hat = NULL,
                            tau_x_method = c("quantile", "outcome_mean")) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_nuisance"))

  tau_x_method <- match.arg(tau_x_method)

  if (is.null(tau_hat)) {
    tau_hat <- estimate_tau_aipw(cf)$estimate
  }

  tau_x_hat <- get_tau_x(cf, tau_x_method = tau_x_method)
  centered_tau_x <- tau_x_hat - tau_hat

  contribution_i <- (
    centered_tau_x^2 +
      2 * centered_tau_x * cf$R_tau
  )

  estimate <- mean(contribution_i)

  list(
    estimate = estimate,
    tau_hat = tau_hat,
    tau_x_hat = tau_x_hat,
    contribution = contribution_i,
    influence = contribution_i - estimate,
    se = standard_error_from_if(contribution_i - estimate)
  )
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# V[bar Delta(U)]
#
# Define
#
#   b_hat(u) = bar Delta_hat(u) - tau_center.
#
# `centering = "aipw"` matches your displayed estimator, using the AIPW
# estimator tau_hat as tau_center.
#
# `centering = "quantile"` uses
#
#   tau_center = int bar Delta_hat(u) du,
#
# and therefore preserves exact centering of b_hat over the grid.
# This is useful for verifying the finite-sample ANOVA identity.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
estimate_V_bU <- function(cf,
                          tau_hat = NULL,
                          centering = c("aipw", "quantile")) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_nuisance"))

  centering <- match.arg(centering)

  if (is.null(tau_hat)) {
    tau_hat <- estimate_tau_aipw(cf)$estimate
  }

  tau_center <- if (centering == "aipw") {
    tau_hat
  } else {
    cf$tau_quantile
  }

  b_hat <- cf$bar_delta - tau_center

  plug_in <- integrate_u(b_hat^2, cf$w)

  score_correction_i <- 2 * integrate_u(
    sweep(cf$eta_diff, 2L, b_hat, FUN = "*"),
    cf$w
  )

  estimate <- plug_in + mean(score_correction_i)

  # A clean empirical influence-function standard error is supplied for
  # quantile centering, where int b_hat(u) du = 0 by construction.
  if (centering == "quantile") {
    x_component_i <- 2 * (
      integrate_u(
        sweep(cf$delta, 2L, b_hat, FUN = "*"),
        cf$w
      ) - plug_in
    )

    influence <- x_component_i + score_correction_i
    se <- standard_error_from_if(influence)
  } else {
    influence <- rep(NA_real_, length(cf$Y))
    se <- NA_real_
  }

  list(
    estimate = estimate,
    plug_in = plug_in,
    correction = mean(score_correction_i),
    b_hat = b_hat,
    tau_center = tau_center,
    score_correction = score_correction_i,
    influence = influence,
    se = se
  )
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Interaction component
#
# c_hat(x,u) =
#   Delta_hat(x,u) -
#   tau_hat(x) -
#   bar Delta_hat(u) +
#   tau_center.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
make_c_hat <- function(cf,
                       tau_x_method = c("quantile", "outcome_mean"),
                       tau_center) {
  tau_x_method <- match.arg(tau_x_method)

  tau_x_hat <- get_tau_x(cf, tau_x_method = tau_x_method)

  c_hat <- sweep(cf$delta, 1L, tau_x_hat, FUN = "-")
  c_hat <- sweep(c_hat, 2L, cf$bar_delta, FUN = "-")
  c_hat <- c_hat + tau_center

  c_hat
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# V[c(X,U)]
#
# V_hat[c(X,U)] =
#   n^{-1} sum_i int [
#     c_hat(X_i,u)^2 +
#     2 c_hat(X_i,u) {eta_hat_1(Z_i,u) - eta_hat_0(Z_i,u)}
#   ] du.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

estimate_V_cXU <- function(cf,
                           tau_hat = NULL,
                           tau_x_method = c("quantile", "outcome_mean"),
                           centering = c("aipw", "quantile")) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_nuisance"))

  tau_x_method <- match.arg(tau_x_method)
  centering <- match.arg(centering)

  if (is.null(tau_hat)) {
    tau_hat <- estimate_tau_aipw(cf)$estimate
  }

  tau_center <- if (centering == "aipw") {
    tau_hat
  } else {
    cf$tau_quantile
  }

  c_hat <- make_c_hat(
    cf = cf,
    tau_x_method = tau_x_method,
    tau_center = tau_center
  )

  plug_in_i <- integrate_u(c_hat^2, cf$w)

  correction_i <- 2 * integrate_u(
    c_hat * cf$eta_diff,
    cf$w
  )

  contribution_i <- plug_in_i + correction_i
  estimate <- mean(contribution_i)
  
  list(
    estimate = estimate,
    plug_in = mean(plug_in_i),
    correction = mean(correction_i),
    c_hat = c_hat,
    tau_center = tau_center,
    contribution = contribution_i,
    influence = contribution_i - estimate,
    se = standard_error_from_if(contribution_i - estimate)
  )
}


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pure plug-in decomposition
#
# This is included as a diagnostic. It uses a common quantile-based centering:
#
#   tau_q = mean_i int Delta_hat(X_i,u) du
#
# and should satisfy the discrete ANOVA identity up to floating-point error.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
plugin_decomposition <- function(cf) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_nuisance"))

  tau_x <- cf$tau_x_quantile
  tau_q <- cf$tau_quantile

  b_hat <- cf$bar_delta - tau_q

  c_hat <- make_c_hat(
    cf = cf,
    tau_x_method = "quantile",
    tau_center = tau_q
  )

  psi2 <- mean(integrate_u(cf$delta^2, cf$w))
  tau2 <- tau_q^2
  V_tauX <- mean((tau_x - tau_q)^2)
  V_bU <- integrate_u(b_hat^2, cf$w)
  V_cXU <- mean(integrate_u(c_hat^2, cf$w))

  decomposition_sum <- tau2 + V_tauX + V_bU + V_cXU

  list(
    Psi2 = psi2,
    tau = tau_q,
    tau2 = tau2,
    V_tauX = V_tauX,
    V_bU = V_bU,
    V_cXU = V_cXU,
    decomposition_sum = decomposition_sum,
    decomposition_gap = psi2 - decomposition_sum
  )
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Full one-step decomposition
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

estimate_wasserstein_decomposition <- function(
    cf,
    tau_x_method = c("quantile", "outcome_mean"),
    centering = c("aipw", "quantile")) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_nuisance"))

  tau_x_method <- match.arg(tau_x_method)
  centering <- match.arg(centering)

  psi2_fit <- estimate_Psi2(cf)
  tau_fit <- estimate_tau_aipw(cf)

  tau2_fit <- list(
    estimate = tau_fit$estimate^2,
    influence = 2 * tau_fit$estimate * tau_fit$influence,
    se = standard_error_from_if(
      2 * tau_fit$estimate * tau_fit$influence
    )
  )

  V_tauX_fit <- estimate_V_tauX(
    cf = cf,
    tau_hat = tau_fit$estimate,
    tau_x_method = tau_x_method
  )

  V_bU_fit <- estimate_V_bU(
    cf = cf,
    tau_hat = tau_fit$estimate,
    centering = centering
  )

  V_cXU_fit <- estimate_V_cXU(
    cf = cf,
    tau_hat = tau_fit$estimate,
    tau_x_method = tau_x_method,
    centering = centering
  )

  decomposition_sum <- (
    tau2_fit$estimate +
      V_tauX_fit$estimate +
      V_bU_fit$estimate +
      V_cXU_fit$estimate
  )

  list(
    Psi2 = psi2_fit,
    tau = tau_fit,
    tau2 = tau2_fit,
    V_tauX = V_tauX_fit,
    V_bU = V_bU_fit,
    V_cXU = V_cXU_fit,

    one_step_decomposition_sum = decomposition_sum,
    one_step_decomposition_gap = (
      psi2_fit$estimate - decomposition_sum
    ),

    plugin_check = plugin_decomposition(cf)
  )
}
```

```{r}
# FOR EXAMPLE IMPLEMENTATION, MAKES estimate_terms FUNCTION 
library(quantreg)

set.seed(42)

n <- 3000
u_grid <- seq(0.02, 0.98, length.out = 49)

estimate_terms <- function(dat) {
  X_mat <- model.matrix(~ factor(X), data = dat)

  cf <- crossfit_wasserstein_nuisance(
    X = X_mat,
    A = dat$A,
    Y = dat$Y,
    u = u_grid,
    K = 5,
    add_intercept = FALSE,
    rearrange = TRUE,
    seed = 2026
  )

  fit <- estimate_wasserstein_decomposition(
    cf,
    tau_x_method = "quantile",
    centering = "aipw"
  )

  data.frame(
    term = c(
      "Psi2",
      "tau",
      "tau2",
      "V_tauX",
      "V_bU",
      "V_cXU",
      "sum_of_terms",
      "gap"
    ),
    estimate = c(
      fit$Psi2$estimate,
      fit$tau$estimate,
      fit$tau2$estimate,
      fit$V_tauX$estimate,
      fit$V_bU$estimate,
      fit$V_cXU$estimate,
      fit$one_step_decomposition_sum,
      fit$one_step_decomposition_gap
    )
  )
}

```

```{r}
# EXAMPLE 1 SIMULTATIONS

X <- sample(1:3, size = n, replace = TRUE)
A <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.5)

mu0 <- c(20, 30, 40)
sd0 <- c(1, 1, 1)

mu1 <- c(30, 40, 50)
sd1 <- c(1, 1, 1)

Y <- rnorm(
  n,
  mean = ifelse(A == 1, mu1[X], mu0[X]),
  sd   = ifelse(A == 1, sd1[X], sd0[X])
)

dat1 <- data.frame(X = X, A = A, Y = Y)

case1_results <- estimate_terms(dat1)
case1_results

```

```{r}
# EXAMPLE 2 SIMULATION

X <- sample(1:3, size = n, replace = TRUE)
A <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.5)

mu0 <- c(20, 30, 40)
sd0 <- c(1, 1, 1)

mu1 <- c(10, 30, 50)
sd1 <- c(3, 3, 3)

Y <- rnorm(
  n,
  mean = ifelse(A == 1, mu1[X], mu0[X]),
  sd   = ifelse(A == 1, sd1[X], sd0[X])
)

dat2 <- data.frame(X = X, A = A, Y = Y)

case2_results <- estimate_terms(dat2)
case2_results
```

```{r}
# EXAMPLE 3 SIMULATION
X <- sample(1:3, size = n, replace = TRUE)
A <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.5)

mu0 <- c(20, 30, 40)
sd0 <- c(1, 1, 1)

mu1 <- c(10, 30, 50)
sd1 <- c(1, 2, 3)

Y <- rnorm(
  n,
  mean = ifelse(A == 1, mu1[X], mu0[X]),
  sd   = ifelse(A == 1, sd1[X], sd0[X])
)

dat3 <- data.frame(X = X, A = A, Y = Y)

case3_results <- estimate_terms(dat3)
case3_results

```




```{r}
# EXAMPLE 1 VISUAL

library(plotly)

set.seed(123)



n_per_group <- 200

species <- rep(1:3, each = 2 * n_per_group)

soil_type <- rep(
  rep(c(1, 0), each = n_per_group),
  times = 3
)

p_11 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 1)
p_10 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 20, sd = 1)

p_21 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 40, sd = 1)
p_20 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 1)

p_31 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 50, sd = 1)
p_30 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 40, sd = 1)

p <- c(
  p_11, p_10,
  p_21, p_20,
  p_31, p_30
)

dat <- data.frame(
  species = factor(species),
  soil_type = factor(soil_type),
  p = p
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Horizontal positions:
# large gaps between species, small gaps within species
# ------------------------------------------------------------

species_x <- c("1" = 1, "2" = 4, "3" = 7)

soil_offset <- c(
  "0" = -0.28,
  "1" =  0.28
)

soil_colors <- c(
  "0" = "royalblue",
  "1" = "red"
)

# Use one common support grid so curves are directly comparable
p_grid <- seq(
  min(dat$p) - 2,
  max(dat$p) + 2,
  length.out = 250
)

fig <- plot_ly()

for (sp in levels(dat$species)) {
  for (soil in levels(dat$soil_type)) {

    values <- dat$p[
      dat$species == sp &
      dat$soil_type == soil
    ]

    dens <- density(
      values,
      from = min(p_grid),
      to = max(p_grid),
      n = length(p_grid)
    )

    x_location <- species_x[sp] + soil_offset[soil]

    # 3D density curve
    fig <- fig %>%
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = dens$y,
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 8
        ),
        name = paste("Soil", soil),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = (sp == "1"),
        hovertemplate = paste0(
          "Species: ", sp,
          "<br>Soil type: ", soil,
          "<br>Outcome: %{y:.2f}",
          "<br>Density: %{z:.3f}",
          "<extra></extra>"
        )
      ) %>%
      # Optional baseline for locating each curve in 3D space
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = rep(0, length(dens$x)),
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 2,
          dash = "dot"
        ),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = FALSE,
        hoverinfo = "skip"
      )
  }
}

fig %>%
  layout(
    title = "Distributional Curves by Species and Soil Type",
    legend = list(
      title = list(text = "Soil type")
    ),
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(
        title = "X",
        tickvals = unname(species_x),
        ticktext = names(species_x),
        range = c(0, 8),
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      yaxis = list(
        title = "Y",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      zaxis = list(
        title = "Density",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      camera = list(
        eye = list(x = 1.5, y = -1.7, z = 1.1)
      )
    )
  )

```


```{r}
# EXAMPLE 2 VISUAL
library(plotly)

set.seed(123)



n_per_group <- 200

species <- rep(1:3, each = 2 * n_per_group)

soil_type <- rep(
  rep(c(1, 0), each = n_per_group),
  times = 3
)

# Treated distributions: variance = 4, so sd = 2
p_11 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 10, sd = 3)
p_21 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 3)
p_31 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 50, sd = 3)

# Untreated distributions unchanged: sd = 1
p_10 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 20, sd = 1)
p_20 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 1)
p_30 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 40, sd = 1)

p <- c(
  p_11, p_10,
  p_21, p_20,
  p_31, p_30
)

dat <- data.frame(
  species = factor(species),
  soil_type = factor(soil_type),
  p = p
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# x-axis spacing:
# bigger gaps between species, smaller gaps within species
# ------------------------------------------------------------

species_x <- c("1" = 1, "2" = 4, "3" = 7)
soil_offset <- c("0" = -0.28, "1" = 0.28)

soil_colors <- c(
  "0" = "royalblue",
  "1" = "red"
)

# Common support for all density curves
p_grid <- seq(
  min(dat$p) - 2,
  max(dat$p) + 2,
  length.out = 1000
)

fig <- plot_ly()

for (sp in levels(dat$species)) {
  for (soil in levels(dat$soil_type)) {
    
    values <- dat$p[
      dat$species == sp &
      dat$soil_type == soil
    ]
    
    # Smooth kernel density estimate
    dens <- density(
      values,
      from = min(p_grid),
      to = max(p_grid),
      n = 1000,
      adjust = 1.4
    )
    
    x_location <- species_x[sp] + soil_offset[soil]
    
    # Main 3D density curve
    fig <- fig %>%
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = dens$y,
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 8
        ),
        name = paste("Soil", soil),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = (sp == "1"),
        hovertemplate = paste0(
          "Species: ", sp,
          "<br>Soil type: ", soil,
          "<br>Outcome: %{y:.2f}",
          "<br>Density: %{z:.3f}",
          "<extra></extra>"
        )
      ) %>%
      # Baseline beneath each curve
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = rep(0, length(dens$x)),
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 2,
          dash = "dot"
        ),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = FALSE,
        hoverinfo = "skip"
      )
  }
}

fig %>%
  layout(
    title = "Smoothed Distributional Curves by Species and Soil Type",
    legend = list(
      title = list(text = "Soil type")
    ),
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(
        title = "X",
        tickvals = unname(species_x),
        ticktext = names(species_x),
        range = c(0, 8),
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      yaxis = list(
        title = "Y",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      zaxis = list(
        title = "Density",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      camera = list(
        eye = list(x = 1.5, y = -1.7, z = 1.1)
      )
    )
  )
```

```{r}
# EXAMPLE 3 VISUAL
library(plotly)

set.seed(123)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Simulate:
# 3 species × 2 soil types × 200 observations = 1200 total
#
# Soil type 1 = treated (red)
# Soil type 0 = untreated (blue)
#
# Treated variance = 4  => treated SD = 2
# Treated means: 10, 30, 50
#
# Untreated distributions remain the same:
# means: 10, 20, 30
# SD = 1
# ------------------------------------------------------------

n_per_group <- 200

species <- rep(1:3, each = 2 * n_per_group)

soil_type <- rep(
  rep(c(1, 0), each = n_per_group),
  times = 3
)

# Treated distributions: variance = 4, so sd = 2
p_11 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 10, sd = 1)
p_21 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 2)
p_31 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 50, sd = 3)

# Untreated distributions unchanged: sd = 1
p_10 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 20, sd = 1)
p_20 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 1)
p_30 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 40, sd = 1)

p <- c(
  p_11, p_10,
  p_21, p_20,
  p_31, p_30
)

dat <- data.frame(
  species = factor(species),
  soil_type = factor(soil_type),
  p = p
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# x-axis spacing:
# bigger gaps between species, smaller gaps within species
# ------------------------------------------------------------

species_x <- c("1" = 1, "2" = 4, "3" = 7)
soil_offset <- c("0" = -0.28, "1" = 0.28)

soil_colors <- c(
  "0" = "royalblue",
  "1" = "red"
)

# Common support for all density curves
p_grid <- seq(
  min(dat$p) - 2,
  max(dat$p) + 2,
  length.out = 1000
)

fig <- plot_ly()

for (sp in levels(dat$species)) {
  for (soil in levels(dat$soil_type)) {
    
    values <- dat$p[
      dat$species == sp &
      dat$soil_type == soil
    ]
    
    # Smooth kernel density estimate
    dens <- density(
      values,
      from = min(p_grid),
      to = max(p_grid),
      n = 1000,
      adjust = 1.4
    )
    
    x_location <- species_x[sp] + soil_offset[soil]
    
    # Main 3D density curve
    fig <- fig %>%
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = dens$y,
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 8
        ),
        name = paste("Soil", soil),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = (sp == "1"),
        hovertemplate = paste0(
          "Species: ", sp,
          "<br>Soil type: ", soil,
          "<br>Outcome: %{y:.2f}",
          "<br>Density: %{z:.3f}",
          "<extra></extra>"
        )
      ) %>%
      # Baseline beneath each curve
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = rep(0, length(dens$x)),
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 2,
          dash = "dot"
        ),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = FALSE,
        hoverinfo = "skip"
      )
  }
}

fig %>%
  layout(
    title = "Smoothed Distributional Curves by Species and Soil Type",
    legend = list(
      title = list(text = "Soil type")
    ),
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(
        title = "X",
        tickvals = unname(species_x),
        ticktext = names(species_x),
        range = c(0, 8),
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      yaxis = list(
        title = "Y",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      zaxis = list(
        title = "Density",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      camera = list(
        eye = list(x = 1.5, y = -1.7, z = 1.1)
      )
    )
  )
```


```{r}
# JOB CORPS EXAMPLE FOR DR. SCHINDL, DR. WASSERMAN
#install.packages('causalweight')
pkg <- "causalweight"
repo <- "https://cloud.r-project.org"
ap <- available.packages(repos = repo)
ver <- ap[pkg, "Version"]
tarball <- tempfile(fileext = ".tar.gz")
url <- sprintf("%s/src/contrib/%s_%s.tar.gz", repo, pkg, ver)
download.file(url, tarball, mode = "wb")
# Extract only the JC data file
exdir <- tempfile()
untar(
  tarball,
  files = sprintf("%s/data/JC.RData", pkg),
  exdir = exdir
)
# Load JC into your R environment
load(file.path(exdir, pkg, "data", "JC.RData"))
dim(JC)
names(JC)



dat <- JC

A <- dat$assignment
Y <- dat$earny4
X <- dat[, c("female", "age", "educ", "everwkd", "mwearn")]

dat_jc <- data.frame(X = X[,"everwkd", ], A, Y)
estimate_terms(dat_jc)
```
